name: Embalse de Pálmaces from Hiendelaencina — circular via the Camino del Cid
name_original: Embalse de Pálmaces desde Hiendelaencina — circular por el Camino del Cid
region_intro: Hiking around Hiendelaencina offers access to varied terrain, including significant peaks, reservoirs, and historical mining areas. The region features a mix of moderate and difficult hiking trails, with no easy routes listed. Hikers can explore landscapes ranging from elevated viewpoints to circular paths around water bodies and through areas with historical significance. Best hiking trails around Hiendelaencina The most popular hiking route is Viewpoint to the Bear Cave from Prádena de Atienza, a 6.0 miles (9.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 38 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views towards a notable cave. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pico del Alto Rey — circular via the old path of Alto Rey from Bustares, a difficult 10.2 miles (16.4 km) path. This trail ascends to a prominent peak, providing expansive views. Local hikers also love the Embalse de Pálmaces from Hiendelaencina — circular via the Camino del Cid, a 15.9 miles (25.5 km) trail leading through reservoir landscapes, often completed in about 7 hours 17 minutes. Hiking around Hiendelaencina is defined by significant peaks, reservoir circuits, and historical mining areas. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels. The routes in Hiendelaencina are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 8 reviews. More than 40 hikers have used komoot to explore Hiendelaencina's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: Are there any easy hiking trails around Hiendelaencina?
A: The hiking routes directly around Hiendelaencina are generally moderate to difficult, with no easy trails listed in our current selection. The terrain often involves significant elevation changes and longer distances, making them more suitable for hikers with some experience.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Hiendelaencina?
A: Hikers in Hiendelaencina can expect varied terrain, including paths leading to significant peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4187789" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Rey Peak (1,858 m)&lt;/a&gt;, circuits around reservoirs such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5388806" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañamares River Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5855101" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcorlo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and routes through areas with historical significance, particularly related to mining.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Hiendelaencina?
A: Yes, several routes offer a circular experience. For example, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030383946/pico-del-alto-rey-circular-via-the-old-path-of-alto-rey-from-bustares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Alto Rey — circular via the old path of Alto Rey from Bustares&lt;/a&gt; provides expansive views from a prominent peak. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027319882/embalse-de-palmaces-from-hiendelaencina-circular-via-the-camino-del-cid" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Pálmaces from Hiendelaencina — circular via the Camino del Cid&lt;/a&gt;, which explores reservoir landscapes.

Q: What are the best times of year to go hiking in Hiendelaencina?
A: Spring and autumn are generally ideal for hiking in Hiendelaencina, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can be hot, especially at midday, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended. Winter hiking is possible but requires appropriate gear for potentially cold and snowy conditions, particularly at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Absolutely. Hiendelaencina offers several challenging routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030383946/pico-del-alto-rey-circular-via-the-old-path-of-alto-rey-from-bustares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Alto Rey — circular via the old path of Alto Rey from Bustares&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 16.4 km path ascending to a prominent peak. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027319882/embalse-de-palmaces-from-hiendelaencina-circular-via-the-camino-del-cid" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Pálmaces from Hiendelaencina — circular via the Camino del Cid&lt;/a&gt;, a 25.5 km trail through reservoir landscapes.

Q: What can I see along the hiking trails in Hiendelaencina?
A: Hikers can discover a variety of points of interest. You might encounter historical mining areas, enjoy views towards natural features like the Bear Cave, or explore the surroundings of reservoirs such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5388806" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañamares River Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features the prominent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4187789" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Rey Peak (1,858 m)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any hikes with good viewpoints?
A: Yes, many trails in the region offer excellent viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030368968/viewpoint-to-the-bear-cave-from-pradena-de-atienza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint to the Bear Cave from Prádena de Atienza&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route specifically designed to offer views towards a notable cave. Additionally, the ascent to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4187789" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Rey Peak (1,858 m)&lt;/a&gt; provides expansive panoramic views of the surrounding landscape.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Hiendelaencina?
A: The trails in Hiendelaencina are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 8 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the sense of adventure on the more challenging routes, and the natural beauty of the peaks and reservoirs.

Q: Is there public transport access to the hiking areas around Hiendelaencina?
A: Public transport options directly to the trailheads around Hiendelaencina are limited. It is generally recommended to travel by car to access most hiking routes, especially those starting from smaller villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5454757" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Prádena de Atienza&lt;/a&gt; or Bustares, or directly from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4681084" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiendelaencina&lt;/a&gt; itself.

Q: Where can I park when hiking in Hiendelaencina?
A: Parking is typically available in the villages where the trails begin, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4681084" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiendelaencina&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5454757" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Prádena de Atienza&lt;/a&gt;. It's advisable to check local signage for specific parking regulations and designated areas, especially during peak seasons.

Q: Are there any long-distance trails that pass through Hiendelaencina?
A: Yes, the historic Camino del Cid passes through the region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027319882/embalse-de-palmaces-from-hiendelaencina-circular-via-the-camino-del-cid" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Pálmaces from Hiendelaencina — circular via the Camino del Cid&lt;/a&gt; route incorporates a section of this significant long-distance path, offering a glimpse into its historical journey.

Q: What should I wear for hiking in Hiendelaencina?
A: Given the varied terrain and potential for significant elevation changes, layered clothing is recommended. Sturdy hiking boots with good ankle support are essential. Depending on the season, bring sun protection, rain gear, and sufficient water, especially for longer or more exposed routes.
tips: It is a very interesting town, with a large number of silver mines in its surroundings. In the La Plata museum everything related to the exploitation of these mines is explained. Very close is the Bornova River, whose canyon can be visited in some sections, with a beautiful landscape. There are also good bar and restaurant services.
Reservoir located in the bed of the Cañamares river, within the Spanish municipal area of Pálmaces de Jadraque.
